The Problem

B2B companies are haemorrhaging pipeline to slow response

The research is clear: speed-to-lead is the single highest-leverage variable in inbound conversion. Most revenue teams are failing at it — not from lack of intent, but lack of infrastructure.

100×
Higher conversion rate when a rep responds within 5 minutes versus responding at 30 minutes.
SOURCE: Harvard Business Review
47 min
Average time for a B2B rep to make first contact with an inbound lead. The window is already closed.
SOURCE: InsideSales.com Research
80%
Of inbound leads go untouched due to manual routing failures and zero SLA enforcement.
SOURCE: Drift Conversational Marketing

Before vs. After

What changes when this system is live

The difference between a revenue team with VelocityIQ and one without isn't automation — it's accountability, speed, and zero lead leakage.

Without VelocityIQ
❌
Lead submitted → sits in CRM queue waiting for someone to notice
❌
Rep manually searches LinkedIn to research the company — 5–15 minutes of dead time
❌
Sales manager guesses which rep should take it — no ICP scoring, no load balancing
❌
No SLA, no escalation — if the rep is busy, the lead just waits or disappears
❌
Duplicate contacts created in CRM — messy data, broken attribution
❌
Management has no visibility until they manually pull a report — always looking backwards

System Architecture

The Intelligent Speed-To-Lead Engine 2.0

Two Make.com scenarios running in tandem: an 18-module main engine firing on every form submission, and an 8-module SLA monitor running every 15 minutes to enforce rep accountability.

01
Capture
Tally.so webhook fires on form submit
02
Enrich
Domain lookup → company intelligence
03
Deduplicate
HubSpot search → Create or Update
04
Score
4-signal ICP scoring (0–80 pts)
05
Grade
A / B / C grade assigned inline
↓
06
Route
Territory + load-balanced rep match
07
Notify
Rich Slack card → assigned rep channel
08
Task
HubSpot task — 5-min due date set
09
Track
Airtable SLA log + Sheets row written
10
Escalate
SLA monitor fires breach alerts

Technology Stack

Six tools. One purpose. Zero budget.

Every tool was selected for a specific architectural reason. This is best-of-breed thinking — not all-in-one convenience.

📋
Tally.so
Lead capture with native webhook support. Fires structured JSON to Make.com on every submission — no paid plan required.
Free Tier
⚙️
Make.com
Orchestration layer. All enrichment, scoring, routing, notification, and SLA logic runs through two Make scenarios.
Free Tier
🔶
HubSpot CRM
CRM backbone. 10 custom properties — ICP grade, lead score, assigned rep, routing territory, lead timestamps and response time — written on every contact. SLA Status is tracked in Airtable.
Free Tier
🗂️
Airtable
Database layer for rep roster, territory routing rules, and SLA tracker. Visual enough for ops teams to manage directly.
Free Tier
💬
Slack
Rep alerts and manager escalations via two channels. Rich block-kit cards — full lead context without opening HubSpot.
Free Tier
📊
Google Sheets
Enrichment lookup table and Lead Response Log. Reporting-ready structure for Looker Studio dashboard connection.
Free Tier
Architectural decision: Tally.so was chosen over HubSpot forms because HubSpot's native webhook trigger requires a paid plan. Airtable handles routing rules because its visual table interface makes rep assignments and territory logic immediately legible to any ops team member — not just the builder.

Cost Comparison

What enterprise teams pay for this. What this cost to build.

Revenue Operations tooling is a major budget line for most B2B companies. VelocityIQ replicates the core functionality of a six-figure enterprise stack — at zero ongoing cost.

Solution What it covers Annual cost (3 reps) Tier
LeanData Routing Lead routing and assignment only — no scoring, no SLA, no notifications $40,000+ / yr Enterprise
Salesloft Rhythm Sales engagement + some routing — requires CRM integration setup $4,500 / yr Paid
Salesforce High Velocity Sales Engagement cadences + Einstein scoring — HubSpot not included $2,700 / yr Paid
Chili Piper Distro Inbound lead routing only — no SLA governance, no escalation $900 / yr Paid
Gong Engage Call intelligence + engagement — no routing or SLA enforcement $3,600 / yr Paid
VelocityIQ STL Engine 2.0 Full STL pipeline: enrichment, ICP scoring, territory routing, SLA governance, AI rep briefings, escalation, reporting dashboard $0 / month Free Tier

Enterprise stack equivalent cost: $51,700+ per year. VelocityIQ achieves the same outcome — enrichment, scoring, routing, SLA enforcement, escalation, and reporting — at zero ongoing cost, using best-of-breed free-tier tools that any B2B team can adopt without procurement approval.

Lead Scoring & ICP Grading

Every lead scored before a rep sees it

Four ICP signals evaluated inline using Make.com nested formulas — no external scoring tool. Score drives rep priority, not gut feel.

Signal Scoring Logic Max
Company Size Employee count via enrichment lookup — tiered 10–30 pts 30 pts
Industry Fit SaaS/Tech → Finance → Healthcare → Manufacturing → Other 25 pts
Job Seniority C-Suite/Founder → VP → Director → Manager → Other 25 pts
Intent & Timeline Immediately → Within 1 month → 1–3 months → Exploring 20 pts
A 65–80 pts
Strong ICP Fit
High-priority. Rep must respond within 5 minutes. Manager alerted at breach. Escalation at 15 minutes.
B 40–64 pts
Moderate ICP Fit
Qualified lead. Standard 5-minute SLA. Full escalation sequence applies.
C 0–39 pts
Weak ICP Fit
Processed, logged, and eligible for automated nurture sequence. Lower rep urgency.

SLA Governance

Accountability without micromanagement

Scenario 2 runs every 15 minutes, querying the Airtable SLA Tracker for breach events and firing cross-tool status updates — no manager intervention required.

Pending
Lead created. Rep has 5 minutes to make first contact.
TRIGGER: Form submission
Within SLA
Rep contacted lead inside the 5-minute response window.
TRIGGER: Response confirmed
Breached
No contact after 5 minutes. Manager alerted to #velocityiq-sla-escalations.
TRIGGER: 5-min monitor check
Escalated
Still no contact at 15 minutes. Senior leadership notified.
TRIGGER: 15-min escalation

Build Evidence

This was actually built.

Every tool, every module, every scenario — live in production. Here's the full walkthrough and platform screenshots.

V3 — AI Layer

The next module: AI-generated rep intelligence

Rule-based scoring tells reps who to call. AI tells them what to say. One additional Make.com HTTP module adds a rep briefing and first-touch email draft to every lead card — for free.

In Progress · Gemini Flash API · 1M tokens/day free

A single Make.com HTTP module calls the Google Gemini Flash API with the enriched, scored lead data. The response is parsed into two outputs: a 3-sentence rep briefing and a first-touch email draft. Both are appended to the Slack notification block and stored as a HubSpot contact note — giving reps context before they dial, with zero extra manual research.

Lead scored + graded
→
HTTP: Gemini Flash API
→
Rep Briefing generated
→
Email draft generated
→
Slack card + HubSpot note
Output 1 — Rep Briefing (in Slack card)
"Sarah — A-grade lead from Acme Corp (350 employees, SaaS). Their VP of Revenue is evaluating for Q3 rollout. 'Immediately' intent signal. Lead score: 74. This is a priority call this morning."
Output 2 — First-Touch Email Draft (in HubSpot note)
"Hi Marcus — saw your demo request for VelocityIQ. Revenue ops tooling is exactly what fast-scaling SaaS teams are solving for Q3. Given Acme's size and timeline, I think we can show you something relevant in 15 minutes. This week?"
Module spec: Make.com HTTP module → POST to https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/models/gemini-1.5-flash-latest:generateContent
Cost: Gemini Flash API — 15 req/min, 1M tokens/day, completely free tier
Placement: Between module 07 (Notify) and module 08 (Task) in Scenario 1

About

Jason Osarebo Adams

Marketing Automation specialist with 2+ years in email marketing and CRM systems, making a deliberate move into Revenue Operations. I built VelocityIQ to demonstrate that RevOps capability is proven through systems — not years on a job title. This portfolio is the evidence.

My background in email marketing gave me a deep understanding of how data moves through a marketing stack and where the handoff between marketing and sales breaks down. VelocityIQ is the architectural answer to that breakdown — a production-grade Speed-To-Lead engine that enforces accountability, eliminates manual routing and gives leadership real-time visibility across the full revenue pipeline.
